Bio: Powerful coast political kingpin and Minister for Local Government who died suddenly while on an official state trip to Germany.
Prominent coast lawyer who won the high-stakes 2004 mini-poll under the National Liberal Party banner, beating the preferred NARC candidate.
Long-serving Vice President, Minister for Internal Security, and mathematics professor who tragically died in a police chopper crash in the Ngong hills.
Former administrator who captured the seat on a TNA ticket, cementing Uhuru Kenyatta's new party dominance right before the 2013 general elections.
Acclaimed Senior Counsel, former Minister for Justice and Constitutional Affairs, and the historic pioneer Senator of Makueni County.
High Court advocate who succeeded his father in a landslide by-election victory, later transitioning to become Makueni Governor in 2022.
Beloved legislator highly celebrated across Kenya for his transformational educational projects and transparent management of the CDF kitty.
Served as the constituency office manager under his late brother, winning the highly contested 2019 mini-poll to preserve the family's development legacy.
Influential Chairperson of the National Assembly Departmental Committee on Administration and National Security who succumbed to illness during his term.
Grassroots youth leader who won a historic, razor-thin proxy battle for the United Democratic Alliance party, shifting the region's political alignment.
Veteran politician and former Leader of Majority who resigned his parliamentary seat to take up his executive appointment as Cabinet Secretary for Defence.
Prominent local leader and businessman who won the subsequent by-election to retain the seat cleanly under the ruling party's banner.
Renowned human rights lawyer, multi-party democracy activist, and Assistant Minister for Internal Security who died alongside other lawmakers on a peace mission.
Son of the late Mirugi Kariuki who contested the seat at age 27, winning on a sympathy wave to serve out the remainder of his late father's term.
Newly elected MP in the December 2007 polls who was tragically shot dead outside his Nairobi home in January 2008, sparking widespread urban protests.
Aggressive grassroots campaigner who won the tense 2008 mini-poll, launching his long career as a legislator and subsequent Kiambu County Governor.
First-time legislator and Assistant Minister for Home Affairs who tragically died alongside Roads Minister Kipkalya Kones in a June 2008 light aircraft crash.
Academic and sister to Lorna Laboso who successfully held the seat for ODM, later rising to Deputy Speaker and pioneer female Bomet Governor.
Prominent transport entrepreneur and Government Chief Whip whose 2007 election victory was nullified by the High Court due to tallying irregularities.
Flamboyant businessman who bypassed traditional major parties to secure a commanding victory on a GNU ticket, paving his path to becoming Kiambu Governor.
Retired Major General who surrendered his parliamentary opposition seat to accept an executive appointment as Cabinet Secretary for Interior.
Switched from the ruling alliance to the opposition ODM right before the by-election, successfully keeping the crucial pastoralist seat in opposition hands.
Highly influential Rift Valley political strategist who resigned his Senate seat after being appointed Cabinet Secretary for Energy and Petroleum.
Youthful political newcomer who overcame a stiff regional challenge to retain the seat, later ascending to become the Senate Majority Leader.
Highly vocal Member of County Assembly who chaired the Finance Committee and was known for challenging local executive governance transparency.
Former media correspondent who staged a strong independent grassroots campaign to defeat the heavily backed ruling party candidate.
